title: Layout Managers, Resize, Scaling, High DPI, and Reflow · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for layout managers, resize, scaling, high dpi, and reflow and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for responsive desktop layout and resize policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.
Derive cell size and offsets from current available space, grid dimensions, minimum target, and aspect policy.
Handle configure events with bounded coalescing and redraw only from committed current dimensions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Small Window Recovery
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Small Window Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz puzzle team handles case 7176 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide a usable minimum, scroll or alternate representation, and reversible recovery when the visual grid cannot fit.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Small Window Recovery.
steps.why: Retrieving Small Window Recovery through the concrete responsive desktop layout and resize policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Small Window Recovery, keep Small Window Recovery connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide a usable minimum, scroll or alternate representation, and reversible recovery when the visual grid cannot fit.
